purpose: Expanded visible panels for Atlas Layers. These are navigation and source-check lanes, not accusations.

people.title: People — current power-watch categories
people.summary: Track living office-holders, institutional executives, public financiers, intelligence leaders, media owners, contractors, scientists, judges, lobbyists, and public influence operators by official role and source lane.

lanes.label: Politicians
lanes.examples: Presidents, prime ministers, EU commissioners, national-security ministers, sanctions officials, migration ministers.
lanes.source: Official government leadership pages
lanes.update: Weekly, or immediately after elections, appointments, resignations, sanctions, or cabinet changes.

lanes.label: Financiers
lanes.examples: Central-bank chairs, IMF and World Bank leadership, asset-management CEOs, sovereign wealth leadership, bank regulators.
lanes.source: Official central-bank, IMF, World Bank, SEC, and company leadership pages

lanes.label: Intelligence figures
lanes.examples: CIA, ODNI, MI6, GCHQ, NSA, defence-intelligence, oversight committee figures, declassification officials.
lanes.source: Official agency leadership pages and hearing records
lanes.update: Weekly; urgent after confirmation hearings or leadership changes.

lanes.label: Media owners
lanes.examples: Media-company owners, chairmen, CEOs, controlling shareholders, platform executives, public editor leadership.
lanes.source: Company leadership pages, filings, ownership disclosures
lanes.update: Weekly; update after mergers, succession, or filing changes.

lanes.label: Contractors
lanes.examples: Defence, surveillance, cyber, cloud, AI, logistics, border, health, and reconstruction contractors.
lanes.source: Company leadership pages, USAspending, EU tenders, investor filings

lanes.label: Scientists and AI leaders
lanes.examples: AI lab executives, research chiefs, standards advisers, biosecurity advisers, health-agency scientists.
lanes.source: Company pages, government advisory rosters, NIST/WHO/OECD lanes
lanes.update: Weekly; urgent after model, standard, or emergency guidance releases.

lanes.label: Judges
lanes.examples: Supreme court, constitutional court, international court, appellate, tribunal, and judge-panel figures.
lanes.source: Court biography, docket, opinion, and hearing pages

lanes.label: Lobbyists
lanes.examples: Registered lobbyists, lobbying firms, foreign agents, trade-association leadership, policy campaign operators.
lanes.source: LDA, FARA, EU Transparency Register, company disclosures

lanes.label: Fixers and influence operators
lanes.examples: Only public-record roles: registered agents, public intermediaries, consultants, crisis-communications firms, legal representatives, donor-network organizers.
lanes.source: Court records, lobbying filings, FARA, public contracts, official disclosures

institutions.title: Institutions — source lanes to map
institutions.summary: Track agencies, NGOs, banks, foundations, universities, media groups, courts, contractors, think tanks, and standards bodies as institutional source lanes.

lanes.label: Agencies
lanes.examples: CIA, NSA, ODNI, DHS, DoD, FDA, CDC, Home Office, Frontex, Europol.
lanes.source: Official agency pages, reports, reading rooms, inspector-general pages
lanes.update: Weekly; daily during emergency or release events.

lanes.label: NGOs
lanes.examples: Humanitarian groups, policy NGOs, rights groups, migration groups, health NGOs, open-government groups.
lanes.source: Official reports, annual reports, funding disclosures

lanes.label: Banks and central banks
lanes.examples: Federal Reserve, ECB, BIS, IMF, World Bank, major banks, development banks.
lanes.source: Official reports, financial statements, stress tests, lending reports

lanes.label: Foundations
lanes.examples: Large philanthropic foundations, policy foundations, donor-advised vehicles, research funders.
lanes.source: Official grants databases, annual reports, tax filings where available
lanes.update: Weekly or quarterly depending on filing cadence.

lanes.label: Universities
lanes.examples: Research labs, AI centres, defence-funded research, public-health labs, policy schools.
lanes.source: University grant, lab, publication, and donor pages

lanes.label: Media groups
lanes.examples: News agencies, broadcaster groups, platform companies, publisher groups, ownership structures.
lanes.source: Company pages, regulator filings, ownership disclosures

lanes.label: Courts
lanes.examples: Supreme courts, international courts, federal courts, tribunals, claims courts.
lanes.source: Court docket, opinion, order, and judgment pages
lanes.update: Weekly; daily during active case developments.

lanes.label: Contractors
lanes.examples: Defence, intelligence, cloud, AI, logistics, private security, medical, and emergency contractors.
lanes.source: Procurement portals, investor pages, contract notices

lanes.label: Think tanks and standards bodies
lanes.examples: RAND, Brookings, CSIS, Chatham House, NIST, ISO, IEEE, OECD, WEF.
lanes.source: Reports, standards, funder pages, event pages

operations.title: Operations — live public-record lanes
operations.summary: Track wars, scandals, trials, covert-project allegations only where public records exist, sanctions, intelligence programs, procurement lanes, and public investigations.

lanes.label: Wars
lanes.examples: Ukraine, Gaza, Red Sea, Iran-linked escalation, Taiwan Strait pressure, Sahel conflict, civil wars.
lanes.source: Official defence statements, UN OCHA, ICRC, court records, sanctions pages
lanes.update: Daily during active escalation; otherwise weekly.

lanes.label: Scandals
lanes.examples: Public-record scandals with court filings, regulator reports, settlement documents, oversight records.
lanes.source: Court dockets, regulator releases, committee reports

lanes.label: Trials
lanes.examples: Criminal trials, civil suits, depositions, exhibits, appeals, sentencing, settlement hearings.
lanes.source: Court dockets, official judgments, PACER/CourtListener where applicable

lanes.label: Covert-project records
lanes.examples: Only documented declassified programs, FOIA releases, reading-room files, inspector-general records, public admissions.
lanes.source: CIA Reading Room, National Archives, agency FOIA libraries

lanes.label: Sanctions
lanes.examples: OFAC, EU, UK, UN sanctions, export controls, asset freezes, designations, licences.
lanes.source: Official sanctions lists and notices

lanes.label: Intelligence programs
lanes.examples: Surveillance law, cybersecurity advisories, declassified capabilities, oversight hearings, inspector-general reports.
lanes.source: Agency reports, congressional hearings, court rulings

lanes.label: Procurement lanes
lanes.examples: Weapons systems, cloud contracts, AI contracts, border technology, medical purchasing, reconstruction contracts.
lanes.source: USAspending, EU tenders, procurement notices, company filings

lanes.label: Public investigations
lanes.examples: Congressional inquiries, public inquiries, inspector-general reviews, regulator investigations, watchdog reports.
lanes.source: Official inquiry and committee pages
lanes.update: Weekly; urgent after hearing or report release.

money-flows.title: Money Flows — traceable public money lanes
money-flows.summary: Track donations, contracts, grants, lobbying, foundations, procurement, shell structures where records exist, settlements, fines, and compensation schemes.

lanes.label: Donations
lanes.examples: Campaign donations, PAC money, foundation gifts, university gifts, donor networks.
lanes.source: FEC, OpenSecrets, tax filings, foundation reports, university disclosures

lanes.label: Contracts
lanes.examples: Defence, intelligence, cyber, AI, healthcare, border, logistics, reconstruction, emergency purchasing.
lanes.source: USAspending, EU tenders, national procurement portals

lanes.label: Grants
lanes.examples: Research grants, development grants, NGO grants, public-health grants, education and technology grants.
lanes.source: Grants.gov, EU Funding & Tenders, World Bank projects, foundation databases

lanes.label: Lobbying
lanes.examples: Lobbying clients, firms, issue areas, bills, agencies contacted, foreign-principal filings.
lanes.source: LDA, FARA, EU Transparency Register

lanes.label: Foundations
lanes.examples: Large foundation grants, policy grants, health grants, media grants, academic grants.
lanes.source: Foundation annual reports, grants databases, tax filings

lanes.label: Procurement
lanes.examples: Who buys, who receives, what category, which emergency authority, which framework contract.
lanes.source: Public procurement databases

lanes.label: Shell structures
lanes.examples: Only when court records, sanctions records, corporate registries, or regulator filings identify them.
lanes.source: Court exhibits, corporate registries, sanctions lists, regulator filings

lanes.label: Settlements and fines
lanes.examples: DOJ, SEC, FTC, EU, CMA, court settlements, admissions, penalties, compensation funds.
lanes.source: Regulator and court releases

lanes.label: Compensation schemes
lanes.examples: Vaccine compensation, disaster compensation, settlement funds, claims facilities, victim funds.
lanes.source: Official claims/compensation programs and court records

legal-records.title: Legal Records — evidence hierarchy lanes
legal-records.summary: Track indictments, civil suits, depositions, hearings, exhibits, judgments, congressional records, FOIA releases, and regulator findings with evidence labels.

lanes.label: Indictments
lanes.examples: Charges, counts, defendants, prosecutors, alleged conduct, dates, case numbers.
lanes.source: Court dockets and DOJ releases

lanes.label: Civil suits
lanes.examples: Complaints, parties, allegations, responses, settlements, dismissals, judgments.
lanes.source: Court records, PACER, state courts, public dockets

lanes.label: Depositions and exhibits
lanes.examples: Transcript excerpts, exhibit numbers, admitted documents, sealed/unsealed status.
lanes.source: Court filings and exhibit lists
lanes.update: Weekly and never without source boundary.

lanes.label: Hearings
lanes.examples: Congressional hearings, public inquiries, oversight hearings, regulator testimony, court hearings.
lanes.source: Committee pages, transcripts, video, court minutes

lanes.label: Judgments
lanes.examples: Court orders, opinions, sentencing, injunctions, appellate rulings, tribunal judgments.
lanes.source: Court websites and official opinions

lanes.label: Congressional records
lanes.examples: Bills, reports, testimony, oversight findings, committee letters, subpoenas.
lanes.source: Congress.gov and committee pages

lanes.label: FOIA and declassification
lanes.examples: Released files, redactions, reading-room records, inspector-general reports.
lanes.source: FOIA libraries, agency reading rooms, National Archives

lanes.label: Regulator findings
lanes.examples: SEC, FTC, DOJ, EU, CMA, data-protection authorities, audit bodies.
lanes.source: Official regulator releases and orders

symbolic-layer.title: Symbolic Layer — public symbolism without overclaiming
symbolic-layer.summary: Track mission patches, logos, ritual architecture, fraternal claims, occult aesthetics, language systems, and public symbolism as commentary lanes, not proof of conduct.

lanes.label: Mission patches
lanes.examples: NASA, NRO, military, intelligence, space, and classified-program public patches.
lanes.source: Official image libraries and agency media pages

lanes.label: Logos
lanes.examples: Agency seals, corporate marks, NGO marks, think-tank marks, institutional visual identity.
lanes.source: Official brand and media-kit pages

lanes.label: Ritual architecture
lanes.examples: Courts, capitals, memorials, lodges, temples, monuments, ceremonial public spaces.
lanes.source: Official heritage, building, and institution pages

lanes.label: Fraternal claims
lanes.examples: Only public lodge claims, official fraternal pages, public member lists, historical records.
lanes.source: Official fraternal/public archive pages
lanes.update: Weekly and never infer private membership.

lanes.label: Occult aesthetics
lanes.examples: Mythic names, esoteric imagery, alchemical language, Egyptian/solar/temple aesthetics, ceremonial design.
lanes.source: Official images plus labelled commentary

lanes.label: Language systems
lanes.examples: Resilience, security, emergency, transformation, misinformation, reform, trust, sustainability, risk.
lanes.source: Official speeches, reports, policy documents

lanes.label: Public symbolism
lanes.examples: Ceremonies, flags, seals, mottos, inscriptions, mythic references, public rituals.
lanes.source: Official public pages and archives
lanes.update: Weekly with clear boundary between fact and interpretation.

human-cost.title: Human Cost — impact lanes and category boundaries
human-cost.summary: Track civilian casualties, migration, exploitation, vaccine compensation, displacement, food stress, medical collapse, and war effects with category definitions before figures.

lanes.label: Civilian casualties
lanes.examples: Deaths, injuries, missing, infrastructure destruction, undercount warnings, methodology differences.
lanes.source: UN OCHA, ICRC, official ministries, court and NGO datasets
lanes.update: Daily during active war; otherwise weekly.

lanes.label: Migration
lanes.examples: Border encounters, asylum applications, removals, returns, overstays, deaths/missing, displacement.
lanes.source: CBP/DHS, Frontex, Eurostat, Home Office, UNHCR, IOM

lanes.label: Exploitation
lanes.examples: Forced labour, trafficking, child exploitation datasets, enforcement cases, support-service reports.
lanes.source: ILO, UNODC, courts, official enforcement pages

lanes.label: Vaccine compensation
lanes.examples: Claims, compensated cases, denied cases, pending claims, legal standards, causation boundaries.
lanes.source: Official compensation programs and court records

lanes.label: Displacement
lanes.examples: Refugees, IDPs, stateless persons, returns, shelter needs, route pressure.
lanes.source: UNHCR, IDMC, IOM

lanes.label: Food stress
lanes.examples: IPC phases, famine warnings, crop failure, price shocks, fertilizer pressure, aid-access disruption.
lanes.source: WFP, FAO, FEWS NET, IPC

lanes.label: Medical collapse
lanes.examples: Hospital pressure, mortality, supply shortages, public-health alerts, preventable harm, emergency declarations.
lanes.source: WHO, national health agencies, AHRQ, official dashboards

lanes.label: War effects
lanes.examples: Casualties, displacement, food stress, infrastructure damage, sanctions spillover, reconstruction cost, unexploded ordnance.
lanes.source: UN agencies, official defence/humanitarian pages, court records
lanes.update: Daily during active escalation; otherwise weekly.
